ranking.one_line: Strongest GROUND (frozen DINO keys + soft-argmax over fixed grid = architecturally cannot emit a memorized constant), but REACH-COMMIT is only borrowed from H7 and inherits its halfway ceiling; needs the co-translation action label grafted in.

Best-articulated barrier diagnosis (InfoNCE-on-in-dist dies like H6, cut-paste is the real escape) and proposes the right 2D-coord/blank-vision decoupling salvage, but seam-shortcut risk is silent and 2D->3D regressor adds a second hidden barrier; GROUND-transfer to seamless swap frames ungated.

Cleanest, most code-grounded build of the cut-paste localizer feeding the H7 channel; killed because the unchanged-action L1 target actively drives loc_proj to be uv-INVARIANT on exactly the paste frames (anti-grounding). Its salvage (co-translate the action through a shared object-agnostic W) is the seed of the winner.

Good distill-localizer engineering and correctly removes the detector from the inference loop, but explicitly breaks REACH-COMMIT (moves coord, keeps original action = trains decoupling) and its salvage requires an offline image->action Jacobian fit that is itself the make-or-break unknown.

EMA/JEPA latent matching is a GROUND-robustness regularizer the author concedes is dominated by plain cut-paste-coord; addresses the WRONG gap (REACH-COMMIT untouched). Salvage is identical to the convergent fix but the JEPA machinery adds compute without buying the missing action label.

Only candidate that targets REACH-COMMIT directly and is fully data-free (no SAM), but the Jacobian/unit-slope constraint leaves the absolute INTERCEPT free -> vision re-memorizes location on swap (reproduces H7 halfway); also slope-1 over an 8-step chunk is physically miscalibrated. Salvageable to an absolute-anchor term but does NO ground.

Feature-space teleport is a clever seamless off-dist GROUND signal, but the GROUND->REACH bridge demands a 2D-grid->3D-metric homography that provably does not exist in the codebase (no depth/intrinsics anywhere) and is forbidden by no-env; each variant (roll vs IDSWAP) sacrifices one of the two gaps.

winner.winner: HYBRID: "CutPaste-Grid-Commit (CPGC)" — content-slot's architectural GROUND head (frozen-DINO-key soft-argmax over a fixed patch grid, supervised by SAM cut-paste off-dist paste centroids) producing a 2D grid-uv coordinate, fed through the existing H7 hard residual channel set to GIBVLA_COORD_DIM=2, AND a REACH-COMMIT term that on cut-paste frames co-translates the demo action laterally by the same image displacement (via a self-supervised image->action lateral Jacobian) so the (real-in-range proprio, off-dist grid-uv coord, off-dist action) triple finally teaches the head to trust coord over proprio. Eval is fully self-contained: swap modeling_prismatic.py:1032 from _gibvla_oracle_coord to the head's own _gibvla_target_pred (no sim oracle, no detector in the loop).
winner.why: Two independent facts force this hybrid. (1) GROUND and REACH-COMMIT are co-equal gaps and NO single candidate closes both: every verdict that killed a candidate did so on the SAME structural error — the GROUND component survives T1 (cut-paste/teleport genuinely puts the named object at off-dist image positions, decorrelating identity from position, which H6's in-dist labels and H8's off-manifold proprio both lacked) but the REACH-COMMIT component either (a) keeps the unchanged demo action as the L1 target on paste frames, which the verified code (finetune_fused.py:392,513 — loss = L1(predicted, batch['actions'])) shows drives loc_proj to be INVARIANT to the paste displacement (copypaste-loc, content-slot, jepa, offline-distill all die here), or (b) regularizes only the Jacobian and leaves the vision-conditioned intercept free to re-memorize (coord-follow), or (c) needs a 2D->3D-metric homography that grep confirms does not exist (wildcard). (2) The winning move is the convergent SALVAGE that four separate adversaries independently arrived at: keep proprio REAL and in-range (avoids H8's off-manifold death — verified P_SCALE x=0.11988 makes Δ=0.10m → ±0.83 normalized), move the signal in IMAGE space, work the coord in the 2D patch-grid frame so loc_proj learns grid-uv->action end-to-end (dodges the meters/pixels trap that killed wildcard and is a hidden second barrier in offline-distill/contrastive), and CO-TRANSLATE the action so the off-dist coord has a non-self-cancelling target. I pick content-slot's GROUND head over copypaste-loc's free-MLP localizer because the verified H6 failure (target_localizer.py: trainable keys + free 3-dim regression MLP target_pred_fc2 → memorized, cos<=0) is exactly what a free localizer reproduces; soft-argmax over a FIXED grid with FROZEN DINO keys removes both memorization routes by construction, so even if cut-paste coverage is imperfect the head cannot emit a per-object constant. CPGC maximizes the objective: moves-swap (both gaps closed), generalizes (content-addressed read + off-dist action label), no-env (SAM/cut-paste offline + image-plane Jacobian), real-transferable (every signal is RGB/proprio), buildable (all plumbing — H7 channel, COORD_DIM, localizer-at-inference, _gibvla_target_pred stash — already exists in code).
winner.winner_spec:
CPGC, warm-started from outputs/object, identity-at-init (loc_proj zero-init = byte-identical step-0, clean holds), ~30 min/1xH100, batch 16, ~2500 steps. ENV: GIBVLA_CPGC=1 GIBVLA_COORD_INJECT=1 GIBVLA_COORD_DIM=2 GIBVLA_CUTPASTE_P=0.5. 

GROUND head (new prismatic/util/cpgc_head.py, mirror target_localizer.py PEFT discipline, force-unfreeze match 'cpgc'): (a) KEYS = DINO-half patch features grabbed pre-FusedFAN-projector in _process_vision_features (modeling_prismatic.py:213 patches_fused, take DINO slice along dim=2) through a tiny EMA key-encoder k_enc (LN+Linear ->128, tau>=0.99, requires_grad=False, stop-grad) — position-equivariant, cannot co-adapt (kills H6 route i). (b) QUERY q = q_proj(language_features) (already at :745). (c) attn = softmax(<q,k>/sqrt(d)) over the 16x16=256 patches; NO value MLP (kills H6 route ii). (d) c_grid = sum attn_ij * g_ij where g_ij is a registered NON-trainable buffer of patch-center (u,v) in [-1,1]^2 — a convex combination of fixed anchors, can only point where attention mass sits. Output c_grid (B,2) is stashed as self._gibvla_target_pred. 

REACH via H7 (no action_heads.py structural change — loc_proj already zero-init, COORD_DIM=2 already supported at :54): set _coord = c_grid in finetune_fused.py:504 (replacing grasp_target) and at modeling_prismatic.py:1032 set _coord = self._gibvla_target_pred (replacing _gibvla_oracle_coord) so eval is self-contained. 

DATA (RLDSBatchTransform.__call__, datasets.py:~144, env-gated): offline precompute (vla-scripts/cpgc_preproc.py) runs GroundingDINO(instruction noun)->SAM per frame, caches RGBA cutout + detected-original centroid keyed by (episode,step). At collate, with prob 0.5: alpha+Poisson-blend the cutout at random (u',v'), set cutpaste_uv = normalized paste centroid; else cutpaste_uv = detected original centroid (supervise originals too). 

LOSSES (finetune_fused.py, reuse equiv_warmup pattern): L = L1(predicted_actions, action_label) + lam_g*L_ground + lam_c*L_commit. L_ground = MSE(c_grid, cutpaste_uv) (lam_g~5, warmup 300). REACH-COMMIT (the load-bearing graft): on the 50% paste frames ONLY, co-translate the action label laterally: action_label[:, :, 0:2] = batch['actions'][:, :, 0:2] + J_img2act @ (uv_paste - uv_orig), where J_img2act is a 2x2 image-uv->action-xy lateral Jacobian fit ONCE offline (scripts/fit_img2act.py: regress recorded delta-eef-xy onto detector-centroid optical-flow of the gripper across the existing RLDS — self-supervised, real-frames-only, no sim); proprio stays REAL/untouched; z/gripper channels unchanged. This makes the paste-frame triple (real proprio, off-dist c_grid, co-translated action) one where proprio-memory is WRONG and c_grid is the ONLY signal consistent with the action -> the missing trust-coord-over-proprio gradient flows through the un-nullable loc_proj. Keep lam on commit so coord-following is not a soft path the L1 head ignores; restrict relabel to lateral xy (the LIBERO swap failure mode). 

BATTERY: swap+task+clean + ImageNet-C control, >=3 seeds, report blank-vision reach (GIBVLA_COORD_BLANK_VISION diagnostic) AND non-blank binary swap success (the real objective). Ablations: cut-paste ON vs OFF (must reproduce H6 memorization when OFF); commit-term ON vs OFF (must reproduce H7 halfway when OFF); EMA-key vs trainable-key (memorization probe).
winner.ground_plan: Close GROUND data-free with the cut-paste content-addressed read, which is the one ingredient H6/H8 lacked. (1) Off-dist generator = SAM cut-paste on EXISTING RLDS frames only: GroundingDINO(instruction noun)->SAM mask offline, cut the named object, Poisson/alpha-blend it at a RANDOM new image (u',v') for 50% of frames. The paste centroid is a self-supervised 2D target the env never rendered — a genuine off-dist object position on real-RGB-compatible ops (T1/T2 pass). (2) The read is architecturally memorization-proof (the content-slot insight verified against H6's failure): FROZEN/EMA DINO keys (position-equivariant, cannot co-adapt) + soft-argmax over a FIXED non-trainable patch-center grid + NO value MLP => c_grid is a convex combination of fixed anchors, so it CAN'T emit a per-object constant unless attention itself is position-independent — which the off-dist paste positions forbid (a per-object constant predictor eats large loss on >=50% of frames). This defeats both H6 collapse routes (trainable keys; free regression MLP) simultaneously. (3) Anti-shortcut: Poisson-blend + photometric match + paste DISTRACTOR (wrong) objects + EMA-teacher keys so the head cannot key on the paste seam; mix 50% un-pasted originals supervised at their detected centroid. (4) Output is the 2D grid-uv coordinate itself (GIBVLA_COORD_DIM=2) — NO 2D->3D lift, dodging the homography that does not exist in the codebase and the hidden-regressor-barrier that weakened offline-distill/contrastive/wildcard. Eval-time GROUND is the same head reading live RGB; swap modeling_prismatic.py:1032 to _gibvla_target_pred so no sim-pose oracle and no detector is in the loop (T4 holds). The only sim-flavored quantity anywhere (grasp_target) is dropped entirely.
winner.reach_plan: Close REACH-COMMIT data-free WITHOUT proprio-OOD by manufacturing the missing off-dist ACTION label in image space. The program-wide blocker (proven by H7's oracle-fed halfway reach 0.105 and H8's off-manifold death) is that on in-dist demos proprio and the correct coord AGREE, so no gradient ever teaches the action to trust coord OVER proprio-memory; and the only data-free way anyone tried (H8: shift proprio by Δ) put normalized proprio-x at ±0.83 and wrecked calibration. The fix (the convergent salvage from copypaste-loc/content-slot/jepa/offline-distill): on the 50% cut-paste frames, keep proprio REAL and in-range, and CO-TRANSLATE the demo action's LATERAL (xy) channel by the same image displacement, action_xy += J_img2act @ (uv_paste - uv_orig). J_img2act is a single 2x2 image-uv->action-xy Jacobian fit ONCE offline by regressing recorded delta-eef-xy onto the detector-centroid optical-flow of the gripper across the existing RLDS (self-supervised, real frames, no sim, no intrinsics). Now the triple (real proprio, off-dist c_grid at uv_paste, co-translated action) is one where the proprio-implied action is WRONG and c_grid is the unique signal consistent with the relabeled action, so the trust-coord-over-proprio gradient flows through the un-nullable loc_proj (T3). This converts the self-cancelling unchanged-action label (which killed copypaste-loc and would cap content-slot at H7's halfway) into a learnable one. Keep the coord in grid-uv space so loc_proj learns grid-uv->action-delta directly (no metric mismatch). Restrict the relabel to lateral xy — the LIBERO swap failure mode the (u,v) coord can physically drive; leave z/gripper to vision+proprio. No env, no re-render, no proprio shift, no privileged pose. Honest residual: if the offline J_img2act fit is too noisy to give a usable lateral label, REACH-COMMIT has no env-free route and the method reduces to GROUND-only — which is exactly what the FIRST experiment de-risks.
winner.first_experiment: Single cheapest decisive test: an ORACLE-COORD + CO-TRANSLATED-ACTION ablation that de-risks the biggest assumption (that an off-dist action label with REAL proprio actually teaches coord-over-proprio through loc_proj) BEFORE building the SAM/GroundingDINO pipeline. Concretely: reuse the EXISTING H7 infra (GIBVLA_COORD_INJECT=1, grasp_target as the coord, loc_proj already there) but add ONLY the co-translation: for 50% of frames sample a lateral Δ in image-grid units, set coord = grasp_target_uv + Δ (project grasp_target to grid-uv once, or just use 2D COORD_DIM=2 with a synthetic Δ), keep proprio REAL/untouched, and relabel action_xy += J_img2act @ Δ using a rough J fit (or even a hand-set lateral gain — this is an oracle test, exact J not needed). Train ~2500 steps, eval swap reach under NON-blank vision with coord = the shifted oracle. DECISION RULE: if swap reach drops BELOW H7's 0.105 toward grasp range (~0.03) AND clean holds, REACH-COMMIT is solvable by image-plane action relabel with in-range proprio -> proceed to build the full CPGC GROUND head and the real J_img2act fit. If swap stays ~0.105 (loc_proj still under-commits even with a correct off-dist action label), REACH-COMMIT is NOT fixed by this signal and the whole program is gated on it -> stop and redesign the commit mechanism (e.g. coord-dropout schedule or up-weighting loc_proj) before spending any time on SAM/cut-paste GROUND. This isolates the load-bearing unknown (does the co-translated action label move the un-nullable channel?) from the GROUND pipeline (whose T1 survival is already the best-established fact across all six verdicts), costs ~30 min and zero new offline preprocessing, and uses only code that already exists.
winner.hybrid_grafts: From content-slot (the GROUND backbone of the winner): frozen/EMA DINO keys + soft-argmax over a FIXED non-trainable patch grid + NO value MLP — the only architecture that provably cannot emit a memorized constant (defeats both verified H6 collapse routes); use it instead of copypaste-loc's free regression MLP. From copypaste-loc and offline-detector-distill: the concrete, code-grounded build plumbing (offline GroundingDINO->SAM precompute cached per (episode,step); collate-time alpha/Poisson cut-paste; force-unfreeze-by-name PEFT discipline; loc_proj COORD_DIM=2; replacing _gibvla_oracle_coord with the head's own _gibvla_target_pred at modeling_prismatic.py:1032 so eval is self-contained and detector-free). From copypaste-loc's salvage: the object-AGNOSTIC single shared image->action map (one W/J, not per-object) so the uv->action mapping cannot be a per-object lookup (blocks the second-order memorization). From contrastive-ground's salvage: (a) GROUND anti-shortcut via consistency — paste the SAME object at K locations and require the matched feature to be INVARIANT across pastes (a seam in every paste is uninformative to an invariance objective), and (b) the H6-metric GATE (hold out a cut-paste split + check soft-argmax tracks the moved object, cos>0, shift-ratio>0.5) before trusting GROUND — make this a hard go/no-go. From coord-follow: keep its unit-slope Jacobian term with vision PRESENT as an OPTIONAL auxiliary to sharpen the grid-uv->reach map, but only layered on top of the absolute co-translated-action anchor (never alone — its intercept-free flaw is fatal solo). From jepa-features: the EMA stop-grad target-encoder for the keys (robustness to paste artifacts) — kept because the EMA key-encoder is independently justified for the GROUND read, NOT as a standalone mechanism.

verdicts.reasoning:
Strongest refutation, grounded in the code. The candidate cleanly SPLITS into two paths and only one survives. GROUND (T1): the SAM cut-paste genuinely puts the named object at off-distribution image positions on real RGB, and the self-supervised paste-centroid target L_loc=MSE(localizer_uv, cutpaste_uv) is satisfiable ONLY by content-attention (a per-object constant predictor incurs large loss on >=50% paste frames). This is a real structural escape from H6 (which trained the localizer on in-dist labels where the prior IS the answer) and from H8 (which perturbed proprio off-manifold). T2 holds: SAM/GroundingDINO run offline on existing RLDS frames; cut-paste is PIL compositing; the localizer target comes from the paste transform, not the sim. So the localizer head will plausibly track the swapped object's uv. That part is sound and novel.

The KILL is the REACH-COMMIT path and it fails T3 by a forced contradiction, not by mere residual risk. Verified in code: the H7 "validated reach 0.105" was obtained with a SIM-POSE ORACLE coord (run_libero_eval.py:414 sets _gibvla_oracle_coord from _gibvla_target_obj_pos(obs,...), the live object xyz from the simulator obs), and even handed the correct answer it only moved halfway. The candidate replaces that oracle with the learned localizer, compounding error. Worse, on the paste frames the action L1 target is the UNCHANGED demo action (gripper/world unchanged) while uv has MOVED to the paste location. The L1-optimal solution is therefore loc_proj(uv_pasted) ~= loc_proj(uv_original) — i.e. loc_proj is driven to be INVARIANT to the paste displacement on exactly the frames that carry the off-dist signal. The candidate's claim ("the only way to keep loc_proj from corrupting the correct action is to encode the displacement consistently") is inverted: encoding the displacement WOULD corrupt the unchanged-action target, so the gradient nulls the channel's response to off-dist uv. This is H2/H6's "L1-optimal head ignores the aux path" reappearing through the hard channel: the H7 channel is un-nullable by ATTENTION but trivially nullable by loc_proj learning a uv-invariant (or per-object-uv-lookup) map, and the paste-frame L1 loss REWARDS that nulling. The two demands — action stays at demo target (correct L1) AND action follows uv shift (reach-commit) — are mutually exclusive on the same frame. The added "reach-commit margin" is itself a soft aux loss (the very thing T3 forbids); to keep clean it must be tiny and cannot overpower the L1 nulling gradient. The only thing wiring uv->action under a non-corrupting target is the in-dist demo frames, where uv is memorizable (the same indistinguishability barrier, now in uv-space) — the candidate's own Risk #2 concedes this. Since a swap SUCCESS (the program's actual objective, not just the localizer metric) needs BOTH gaps closed, and REACH-COMMIT is contradicted by the training objective rather than merely under-hardened, the method does not clearly pass all kill tests. T1 passes for the head but does NOT transfer to the action, so the off-dist signal never reaches the action path. Fails T3; therefore fails overall.
verdicts.salvage: Give loc_proj a NON-CANCELLING off-dist action target so uv->action is trained on genuinely moved positions, env-free. Minimal fix: on paste frames, do NOT keep the action unchanged — CO-TRANSLATE the demo eef-reach action by the SAME image displacement, mapped to action-delta space through a SINGLE shared, OBJECT-AGNOSTIC learned linear map W (2->7, weight-shared across all objects/frames, the ONLY parameter carrying uv->action). Object-agnosticism (one shared W, not per-object) blocks the memorization lookup H6/Risk#2 fear; co-translating the action makes the off-dist uv have a CONSISTENT (non-self-cancelling) L1 target instead of the contradictory unchanged-action target. Keep loc_proj zero-init (warm-start byte-identical) and route localizer_uv->W->residual via the existing H7 channel (un-nullable path, satisfies T3). Critically, also CLOSE THE EVAL LOOP: train loc_proj with the localizer's OWN predicted uv as the coord input on original frames (not the sim oracle), so at swap the action follows the localizer, not a privileged pose. This is pure image-plane compositing + a co-translation computed from the paste transform (no env, no sim pose, no re-render; T2/T4 hold) and is ~30 min warm-start. Residual caveat: the action co-translation is only an image-plane approximation of the true 3D reach delta, so reach-commit will be approximate — but it converts a self-cancelling signal into a learnable one, which is the difference between DEAD and testable.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified against code: H7 channel (prismatic/models/action_heads.py:52-78, 309-310, 441-442) is the un-nullable loc_proj residual exactly as claimed (T3 real); grasp_target is an offline RLDS field (prismatic/vla/datasets/datasets.py:144-145), A_SCALE is in the stats buffer, δ is synthetic, two head-only passes are feasible via predict_action(coord=...) with detach — so T2/T3/T5 pass and the loss-only T4 passes. The off-dist exposure lives in the input coord (±0.18m), not in proprio (avoids the H8 proprio-OOD killer at finetune_fused.py:407-415) and not in env, so T1 is NOT in-dist-fit-satisfiable in the H5/H8 sense. STRONGEST REFUTATION (survives): the loss constrains only the finite-difference JACOBIAN r(c+δ)−r(c)=δ/A_SCALE — the DERIVATIVE of reach w.r.t. coord. Unit slope leaves the absolute map's INTERCEPT b(vision,proprio) free: r(c)=(1/A_SCALE)c+b. On swap eval the in-dist vision tokens (h_t/h_a still show the object at its real/blended position — risks(1)) drive b to the memorized location (the H6 failure), so the arm reaches c_swap+offset even with perfect unit slope. Enforcing the derivative does NOT force the absolute action to commit to the absolute coordinate; it reproduces H7's 'halfway blend' (H7 already consumed the coord, loc_proj nonzero — its failure was the blend ratio of the ABSOLUTE output, which a Jacobian constraint does not touch). The candidate's own mitigation (blank-vision pass-B to kill the vision intercept) collapses to the already-NULL H7 blank regime (swap 0.105). Secondary refutation: r(c)=sum of 8 delta-eef steps; demanding Δ(8-step-sum)=δ/A_SCALE for δ=0.18m forces a full-distance reach within ONE chunk, which is physically wrong for a multi-chunk controller — the true per-chunk gain κ<1, so slope-1 is miscalibrated and will distort in-dist L1 (risks 4), making the 'geometrically exact, no-label' core claim false. Net: passes 5/5 kill tests narrowly but does NOT clearly close its own stated gap (b) REACH-COMMIT (regularizes derivative, not the memorizing absolute intercept) and by design touches neither gap (a). Under 'survives only if CLEARLY closes the gap', it does not clear the bar.
verdicts.salvage: Replace the relative Jacobian target with an ABSOLUTE anchored constraint that pins the intercept, on the blank-vision pass-B (which removes the vision-conditioned intercept that re-memorizes on swap): constrain the absolute cumulative reach toward the injected coord relative to current proprio, r(c) ≈ κ·(c − eef_now)/A_SCALE, where κ is a per-chunk controller gain CALIBRATED on an in-dist held-out reach split (NOT forced to 1, fixing the chunked-displacement miscalibration). Keep the unit-slope Jacobian term with vision present, and add the absolute-anchor term under a small blank-vision fraction, sweeping that fraction. This shapes the absolute coord→reach map (not just its derivative) to follow the absolute coordinate with a geometrically valid gain — no proprio shift (avoids H8), no env, no off-dist action label, identity-at-init preserved via zero-init loc_proj. Still REACH-COMMIT only; a real swap win additionally needs a separate real-RGB GROUND module (detector/Molmo centroid -> coord) to replace the current sim oracle at run_libero_eval.py:334-349/414.

verdicts.reasoning:
VERIFIED against code. The architectural claims hold: the H7 hard residual channel is real and un-nullable (action_heads.py:310/440-442: `x = self.ffn(output + x + loc_token.unsqueeze(1))`, loc_proj zero-init); the DINO half is grabbable pre-projector (modeling_prismatic.py:213 `patches_fused = self.fused_featurizer(img_fused)`, DINOv2, concatenated last along dim=2, so its content is position-equivariant as claimed); language_features exists at :745; and H6's two collapse routes are exactly as the candidate diagnoses (target_localizer.py: TRAINABLE attn keys + a free 3-layer regression MLP target_pred_fc1/fc2 that can emit a memorized 3D constant). So CASR's by-construction critique of H6 is technically correct, and its copy-paste signal genuinely passes T1: it perturbs the IMAGE (where the model reads) not proprio, supplies a self-generated 2D image-space target decorrelated from in-dist cells, frozen DINO keys cannot co-adapt, and soft-argmax over a fixed grid with no value MLP cannot emit a memorized constant unless attention itself is position-independent — which the off-dist paste positions forbid. T1/T2/T3/T4/T5 all pass for the GROUND component.

THE KILL is the REACH-COMMIT gap, which the candidate mislabels as solved ("BOTH ... inherited/strengthened via H7"). The program established (RESEARCH-LOG lines 94-102) that GROUND is NOT the only blocker: even with a perfectly CORRECT coord, H7's oracle gate moved the eef only HALFWAY on swap (swap reach 0.105, drop 0.106) and binary success was ~0 under blank vision, because on in-dist demos proprio and coord AGREE so no gradient ever teaches the action to TRUST coord OVER proprio-memory on swap. The missing ingredient is OFF-DISTRIBUTION ACTION supervision (coord != proprio, action must follow coord). CASR's L_read supervises only c_grid->paste_center — the READ — and supplies ZERO off-dist action signal. On a pasted frame the action label is fatal either way: keep the original demo action and the L1 target is WRONG (only pixels moved, the physical eef/scene is unchanged, so the demo action reaches the original location — training this teaches the action to IGNORE the pasted object); or skip L1 on pasted frames and the action head never sees a single example where coord and proprio disagree. The only valid off-dist action label without env is the relative-reach trick (shift coord AND proprio by Δ, keep action) = exactly H7-CF/H8 (finetune_fused.py:405-413 `cf_coord = grasp_target + delta_m`), which the log shows FAILED because the proprio shift goes off-manifold (Δ=0.10m -> ±0.83 normalized, proprio-x scale 0.12). Therefore CASR's theoretical CEILING is H7's oracle result: a correct coord that under-commits to a halfway swap reach with binary success ~0. Replacing the oracle coord with a learned, noisier content-read can only equal-or-degrade that ceiling, never exceed it, and under NON-blank real eval the proprio-memory shortcut (masked in H7's blank probe) is STRONGER, so the under-committed loc_proj is even more likely overridden on swap. CASR's own risk #3 concedes this ("if loc_proj stays under-committed, swap reach still stalls halfway"). A method that, at its best, reproduces a known halfway-reach with ~0 binary swap success does not move LIBERO-PRO swap toward a real WIN. It solves one of two co-equal gaps and inherits the unsolved one's ceiling.
verdicts.salvage: Add an off-dist ACTION-commit signal that uses real (in-manifold) proprio and the self-generated paste coord, avoiding H8's off-manifold proprio. Minimal fix: on copy-paste frames, keep proprio at its REAL untouched value and add a DIRECTION-only reach-commit loss tying the H7 channel to the read: supervise the predicted action's net displacement direction (a pure function of predicted_actions, H5-style but routed through loc_proj) to align with the unit vector (A(c_grid) - proprio_xyz) — i.e. when the content-read coord and the proprio-implied position DISAGREE (which copy-paste manufactures off-dist, in image space, with no proprio shift), the action must reach toward the read coord. Use direction-only (scale-free) to avoid fabricating exact off-dist deltas, and inject magnitude via the existing un-nullable loc_proj channel (up-weight it / schedule a higher coord-commit lambda) so the commit cannot be a soft path the L1-optimal head ignores. This supplies the missing coord-over-proprio gradient on genuinely off-dist positions without env re-render and without off-manifold proprio (proprio stays real; only the image and a self-generated 2D target move). Validate by checking swap reach drops BELOW H7's 0.105 toward grasp range AND binary swap success rises under NON-blank vision. Without this added REACH-COMMIT term, the GROUND-only CASR is dead at H7's halfway ceiling.

verdicts.reasoning:
I verified the load-bearing infra claims against the code, and the candidate clears the five formal tri-state tests — but it still does not CLEARLY move swap toward a real win, so survives=false (the default).

WHAT IS REAL (tests pass at the literal level):
- T1: SAM cut-paste genuinely puts the named object at NEW image positions with a self-supervised paste-pixel target. This is the one ingredient H6/H8 lacked and is NOT satisfiable by the in-dist fit. The candidate is honest that InfoNCE-on-in-dist alone dies like H6; the cut-paste is the actual barrier-breaker. Confirmed allowed: it is image-aug, not env.
- T2: No env. `_gibvla_target_obj_pos` (the sim-pose oracle in run_libero_eval.py:334) is the H7 stand-in; CPG explicitly replaces it with an RGB pipeline (SAM/OWL-ViT offline on existing frames). Nothing re-renders LIBERO.
- T3: It routes coord through the EXISTING un-nullable H7 channel — verified in action_heads.py:52-78 (loc_proj, zero-init, added to every MLPResNetBlock residual) and modeling_prismatic.py:1032-1037 (coord param wired into predict_action). Not a soft aux path.
- T4/T5: All components (SAM, OWL-ViT, InfoNCE, soft-argmax, (u,v)->xyz) are RGB/proprio ops; warm-start from outputs/object, zero-init backproj keeps clean.

WHY IT STILL FAILS THE 'clearly moves swap' bar — two independent unrefuted gaps:

(A) GROUND-TRANSFER IS UNDEMONSTRATED AND THE PRIOR IS STRONGLY NEGATIVE. CPG's GROUND head reads output.projector_features x language_features — the IDENTICAL cross-attention inputs the H6 TargetLocalizer consumed (target_localizer.py:61-75) and that MEMORIZED (cos<=0, pred-shift 0.03-0.12 vs object-shift 0.18-0.25). The claim is that cut-paste supervision RETRAINS those frozen DINO-SigLIP patch features to become content-grounding. But the cut-paste positive is supervised on COMPOSITED frames with a known paste cell; the head can minimize InfoNCE by detecting the paste ARTIFACT (seam/lighting/inpaint boundary) instead of object content — the candidate's own risk (3). The mitigations (blending, distractor pastes) reduce but do not eliminate this, and the failure mode is silent: the head looks 'content-grounded' on cut-paste val yet still memorizes on real swap, because swap has no seam to key on. There is NO test-time signal that forces the swap-frame p2d to be content-correct; H6 already showed this exact representation does not localize at moved positions. So the GROUND component's generalization to swap is asserted, not gated — and H6 is direct evidence against it.

(B) A SECOND IN-DIST BARRIER IN THE 2D->3D MAP, and a GROUND/REACH regime conflict. H7's swap-moving reach (clean 0.029 / swap 0.105) was measured ONLY under GIBVLA_COORD_BLANK_VISION (modeling_prismatic.py:1116 zeroes the patches) AND with an ORACLE coord; with vision present the head reverts to memorization. CPG's GROUND head NEEDS live patches to compute p2d, so it cannot blank vision at eval — putting REACH-COMMIT back in the regime where H7 never showed the coord overrides proprio (the H7 node's own conclusion: in-dist training cannot teach 'trust coord over proprio'; H8 confirmed forced commitment bought zero swap gain, swap 0.100 ~ 0.105). On top of that, the (u,v)->xyz regressor is itself queried off-dist on swap (new pixels) and can extrapolate wrongly — a regressor-internal version of the same memorization barrier. So even with a perfect p2d, the path p2d->xyz->loc_proj->action is not shown to commit on swap, and the candidate's own risk (5) concedes REACH-COMMIT may stall below H7's halfway.

STRONGEST SINGLE REFUTATION: the method's swap success requires the conjunction (cut-paste retrains features to be seam-independent content-grounders that transfer to artifact-free swap frames) AND (the resulting noisy RGB coord overrides proprio-memory through a channel only ever shown to half-commit under blanked vision with an oracle). Each conjunct is contradicted by an existing result (H6 for the first, H7/H8 for the second). kills_swap_confidence 0.42 is over-optimistic; the realistic expectation is a NULL-to-weak-partial, not a real win. It is a well-motivated experiment worth running as an ablation, but it does not CLEARLY pass the 'moves swap toward a real win' bar, so it does not survive.
verdicts.salvage:
Two-part minimal fix, no env:
(1) GROUND anti-shortcut + held-out swap-style GATE. To stop the InfoNCE head keying on the paste seam, make the contrastive target consistency-based rather than cell-classification: paste the SAME object at K different random locations within a batch and require the matched-patch feature to be INVARIANT across pastes (and distinct from K hard distractor objects) — a seam present in every paste is uninformative to an invariance objective. Critically, ADD A GATE before trusting GROUND: hold out a cut-paste split the head never trained on AND, if any detector works offline, a few real position-shifted frames; pass only if soft-argmax p2d tracks the moved object (cos>0, shift-ratio>0.5) — the exact H6 metric. If it memorizes there, GROUND is dead and the rest is moot.
(2) Decouple GROUND-vision from REACH-vision so REACH-COMMIT stays in H7's validated regime: let the GROUND head read LIVE patches to compute p2d/coord, but feed the ACTION head BLANKED patches + that coord (set GIBVLA_COORD_BLANK_VISION=1 for the L1 path only). This reproduces H7's pure reach-to-coordinate controller (which is the only configuration that moved swap) while using an RGB-derived coord instead of the sim oracle. Then to push past H7's halfway without H8's proprio-OOD bug, schedule coord-dropout on the coord channel (not proprio) so the head must follow coord, keeping proprio in-range. Feed the 2D p2d directly as a 2-D coord (GIBVLA_COORD_DIM=2) to dodge the (u,v)->xyz extrapolation barrier; loc_proj is dimension-agnostic. This is buildable in ~30 min on warm-start and converts the asserted swap claim into a gated, falsifiable one. Even with this, GROUND-transfer (part 1's gate) remains the make-or-break and the prior (H6) is against it.

verdicts.reasoning:
Strongest refutation = the REACH-COMMIT gap, which H7/H8 proved is the BINDING constraint, is not addressed and is actively anti-addressed. The candidate splits into GROUND (novel: cut-paste off-dist localizer) and REACH-COMMIT (borrowed from H7's residual channel). Per-test:

T1 (barrier): PASSES for GROUND only. SAM cut-paste of the named object to random (u',v') genuinely decorrelates identity from position for a fixed object, so the loss-minimizing localizer must content-ground rather than memorize (the exact variable H6 never moved — H6 trained on in-dist frames where identity predicts position). This is a real off-dist RGB signal, not env. Confirmed against H6 node (k2gwp2): in-model TargetLocalizer trained with explicit localization loss shifts only 0.03-0.12 vs object 0.18-0.25, cos<=0 — pure memorization. Cut-paste is a legitimately different supervision distribution.

T2/T4/T5: pass. Verified the H7 hard residual channel is real and consumed (action_heads.py:310/442 `ffn(output + x + loc_token.unsqueeze(1))`, zero-init loc_proj lines 60-61); the localizer already runs at inference and stashes _gibvla_target_pred (modeling_prismatic.py:979-985); the coord-inject path at line 1032 only needs `_gibvla_oracle_coord` swapped for `_gibvla_target_pred`. All ops (GDINO/SAM/composite/distilled localizer) are offline real-RGB, no env. Warm-start identity-at-init holds. Buildable in ~30 min.

T3 (hard path) for the reach: PASSES mechanically (uses the un-nullable H7 residual, not a soft aux). But passing T3 is necessary, not sufficient — and here the channel is fed a coord but the ACTION is never supervised to follow it off-dist.

THE KILL (decisive, survives every salvage that stays env-free): H7 handed the head a PERFECT oracle coord at the true swapped location and STILL reached only halfway (swap min-xy 0.105). H7's own conclusion: in-dist training cannot make coord OVERRIDE proprio-memory on swap, because proprio and coord only disagree off-dist; on in-dist demos they agree, so no gradient teaches trust-coord-over-proprio. A perfect GROUND coord does NOT fix REACH-COMMIT. The candidate's claimed fix ('train loc_proj on cut-paste off-dist coords') is structurally broken: on a cut-paste frame the named object is composited at (u',v') and the localizer target moves, but the recorded ACTION CHUNK and the PROPRIO are still the ORIGINAL in-dist demo (reaching the ORIGINAL object spot). The L1 action loss therefore regresses to an action that reaches the original location while the coord says (u',v') — it trains the head to DECOUPLE/contradict the coord on exactly the off-dist frames (anti-grounding), strictly worse than H7's halfway. To keep the action label valid while moving the coord you need either real proprio at the relocated object (env re-render — forbidden by T2) or an action relabel in a consistent metric space (camera intrinsics/extrinsics, world coords — privileged sim state the candidate explicitly disclaims to dodge H8). Verified in code: H8's GIBVLA_COORD_CF (finetune_fused.py:400-416) is the ONLY data-free way to keep a valid L1 target — shift coord AND proprio by the same delta so relative reach (coord-proprio) is preserved with 'No action relabel' — and it FAILED by putting proprio off-manifold (delta 0.10m -> ±0.83 normalized, scale 0.12). The candidate drops the proprio shift (good for proprio-manifold) but thereby loses the only thing that kept the action label consistent: coord-proprio is now wrong on every cut-paste frame. So GROUND may produce a correct (u,v) on swap, yet REACH stays at H7's 0.105 AT BEST and realistically degrades. The candidate itself concedes this in risk #4 ('inherits H7's reach ceiling') and risk #3 (2.5D->action 'unproven'). A complete swap win needs BOTH gaps closed; this closes at most GROUND and breaks REACH-COMMIT. swap reach 0.105 (halfway, success still 0 — binary success was already 0 under H7's perfect oracle) is not 'a real win'. Secondary concern (not load-bearing): the copy-paste seam shortcut (candidate risk #2) can let the localizer detect the paste blob's boundary rather than object content, which would not transfer to a real seamless swapped object — a known failure mode that further weakens even the GROUND claim.
verdicts.salvage: Add an env-free off-dist ACTION supervision so the L1 loss on relocated frames actually demands coord-following, closing REACH-COMMIT without re-render or privileged pose. Minimal fix: keep the cut-paste GROUND pipeline, but make the relocation a 2D translational counterfactual that preserves a VALID delta-action label WITHOUT touching proprio. Concretely: (1) instead of cut-pasting only the object, apply the SAME 2D image shift (and matching coord shift in (u,v)) to the WHOLE composite for a subset of samples, and relabel the FIRST action-chunk lateral component by the demo-geometry image->action Jacobian estimated self-supervisedly from the existing RLDS (regress recorded delta-eef-xy onto inter-frame optical-flow / detector-centroid motion of the gripper over the dataset — a one-time offline fit on real frames, no sim). This yields a consistent (shifted image, shifted coord, shifted action) triple where proprio stays REAL (only the lateral action target and the coord move together), so on these frames proprio-memory is WRONG and the coord is the ONLY signal consistent with the relabeled action -> gradient finally teaches trust-coord-over-proprio, the exact ingredient H7/H8 said is missing, manufactured on RGB. (2) Restrict the relabel to the lateral (xy) channel the (u,v) coord can drive (depth/z left to vision+proprio), matching the LIBERO swap failure mode (lateral). (3) Keep p_cp>=0.5, Poisson-blend + inpaint + distractors to defeat the seam shortcut, and gate acceptance on a held-out check that localizer accuracy on REAL swap frames tracks cut-paste accuracy. If the offline image->action Jacobian fit is too noisy to give a usable lateral action label, the method has no env-free route to REACH-COMMIT and reduces to GROUND-only (publishable as 'cut-paste breaks localizer memorization' but NOT a swap win) -> then it does not survive.

verdicts.reasoning: Strongest refutation: the candidate addresses the WRONG gap. Verified against the code and nodes. H7 (5i0j20) fed a PERFECT ORACLE coordinate (true sim object world-position, 100% coverage, name-matched, vision blanked) through the validated un-nullable loc_proj residual channel (confirmed live in prismatic/models/action_heads.py: loc_token added at output+x+loc_token in every MLPResNet block, zero-init) and swap reach STILL only moved halfway (0.105, drop 0.106). The H7 conclusion is explicit: in-dist training cannot make the coordinate OVERRIDE proprio-memory on swap because proprio and coord only disagree off-distribution; on in-dist demos they agree, so no gradient teaches 'trust coord over proprio.' That is the REACH-COMMIT gap and it is the actual program blocker. The candidate's GROUND component (cut-paste JEPA predictor of the paste centroid) is a legitimate T1 pass for the PREDICTOR's objective only — but H7 proves a flawless coordinate already existed and was NOT the bottleneck. The candidate's REACH-COMMIT component does not exist: the build_spec only adds jepa_loss + a latent-consistency term and replaces the oracle coord with the predictor's (noisier) coord into the SAME channel; the L1 action loss still trains on the unchanged in-dist (proprio, coord, action) tuples. Cut-paste displaces the object in image space but does NOT relabel the action, does NOT move proprio, and does NOT create a pair where proprio predicts the wrong action and coord is the only corrective signal. So the L1-optimal head still routes the reach through (in-dist-correct) proprio and treats coord as redundant — the exact H7 catch-22 — and feeding a noisier predicted coord risks being worse (H7 flagged this; H8 (ocdaey) degraded clean for the same reason). The claim 'the H7 channel now trained against off-dist coordinates' is false at the action loss: the predictor sees off-dist paste positions, but those frames keep the original in-dist demo actions; there is no off-dist (proprio, action) pair. Why the refutation survives the candidate's own defenses: the EMA/JEPA latent matching only improves GROUND robustness (the risks block already concedes JEPA is a regularizer, not the mechanism), so it cannot touch REACH-COMMIT; and T3 being mechanically satisfied (real un-nullable channel) is necessary but not sufficient — H7 showed the very same un-nullable channel stays under-committed when its coordinate is redundant with proprio in-dist. Net: GROUND addressed (T1 pass for predictor), REACH-COMMIT untouched (same hole as H7/H8) => predicted to be a 9th swap null.
verdicts.salvage: Keep the cut-paste predictor for GROUND, but ADD the missing REACH-COMMIT signal in IMAGE space (so the model stays on-manifold, avoiding H8's proprio-OOD trap): on each cut-paste sample, also synthesize an off-dist ACTION target by geometric relabel toward the paste centroid — set injected coord = unproj(paste centroid) AND replace the L1 action chunk with the translation-invariant delta-reach toward that pasted location, while KEEPING the original frame's real, in-range proprio fixed. Because the paste moves the object in the image but proprio stays real/in-range, this yields a genuine (in-range proprio, off-dist coord, off-dist action) tuple where proprio-memory is WRONG and coord is RIGHT — exactly what H7 said was missing — with no env re-render and without H8's off-manifold proprio perturbation. This is data-free (cut-paste + analytic delta-reach from the known paste centroid), real-transferable, warm-start compatible, and routes through the existing loc_proj channel. Without this action-relabel, the method is a null on swap.

verdicts.reasoning: Strongest refutation: the GROUND->REACH closed loop requires converting a patch-grid (2D image-plane) teleport offset into the 3D workspace coordinate (meters) that the H7 loc_proj channel and L1 reach were trained on. The candidate asserts this conversion is "the fixed image->workspace homography already implied by grasp_target regression." NO such homography exists or is learnable here, and the conversion is forbidden by the NO-ENV constraint. Verified in code: (i) grasp_target is proprio-derived raw eef-xyz at the min-gripper moment (rlds/dataset.py:179-181), so target_pred/loc_proj operate in workspace METERS; (ii) target_pred is a plain MLP regression to xyz (target_localizer.py:77-90), never an invertible image->world map; (iii) grep for homography/extrinsics/intrinsics/depth/backproject across prismatic/ returns nothing — there is no camera calibration or depth anywhere. A 2D image displacement -> 3D metric Delta is under-determined without depth/calibration (privileged geometry the user forbids). The candidate's fallback ("supervise as a 2D-grid offset plus original z") makes GROUND output live in grid/pixel units while loc_proj and the L1 reach consume meters, so L_consist's "expected_delta_from_offset" is either the forbidden calibration or a wrong fronto-parallel pixel->meter constant (LIBERO's primary cam is oblique), injecting systematic reach error. Thus the two gaps never compose into a metric action. Secondary leak (candidate risk a): feature-roll carries stale positional embeddings, so the localizer can fit the teleport seam rather than content; the IDSWAP variant removes the seam but then keeps positions fixed, supplying NO off-dist position signal to the reach channel — so each variant sacrifices one of the two required gaps. T1 genuinely survives (off-dist object positions are manufactured in vision-feature space, decorrelated from per-object priors, unlike H6's in-dist memorizable target and H8's off-manifold proprio corruption), and T2/T3/T4/T5 hold. But because T1 success does not rescue the broken metric GROUND->REACH bridge, the method as specified does not clearly pass all kill tests, so it does not survive.
verdicts.salvage: Keep GROUND and REACH BOTH in the 2D normalized patch-grid frame instead of trying to produce a 3D metric coord. (1) Set the H7 channel to consume a 2D grid location uv in [-1,1] (GIBVLA_COORD_DIM=2) and learn loc_proj: grid-uv -> reach DIRECTLY in pixel space, supervised on IN-DIST frames by pairing the grid centroid of the true object's patches (instruction-patch cosine / offline OWL-ViT) with the existing grasp_target action — no homography needed, the head learns grid-uv->action end-to-end. (2) Feature-teleport then supplies genuinely off-dist grid-uv GROUND targets (label = teleport offset) and L_consist ties the action shift to the GRID offset through the same loc_proj, all in one consistent 2D frame. This deletes the meters/pixels mismatch, preserves T1 (off-dist grid positions), T2/T4 (grid-uv is RGB-computable, no env/depth), and T3 (still the un-nullable H7 channel). Use the seam fix (re-add destination positional embedding to moved tokens) to keep the localizer reading content. Remaining empirical crux to measure: teleport->real-swap feature transfer (H6 cos metric: target_pred shift vs object shift on swap) — confidence stays modest until that is shown.
